problem_addressed: Semi-supervised domain adaptation (SSDA) struggles with scarce semantic label information in target domains, limiting performance of existing approaches.
core_claim: Graph-based Adaptive Betweenness Clustering (G-ABC) achieves superior categorical domain alignment by constructing and refining a reliable heterogeneous graph for semantic propagation.
key_contributions: Heterogeneous graph construction connecting labeled source/target samples with unlabeled target samples
Connectivity refinement via Confidence Uncertainty based Node Removal (CUNR) and Prediction Dissimilarity based Edge Pruning (PDEP)
Adaptive Betweenness Clustering for semantic transfer using pairwise label similarity
novelty: Unlike prior SSDA methods that directly use noisy graphs for semantic alignment, G-ABC introduces explicit graph refinement strategies to degrade unreliable connectivity before clustering.
method_summary: Constructs a heterogeneous graph between labeled source/target samples and unlabeled target samples, refines it using CUNR and PDEP to remove noisy nodes/edges, then applies Adaptive Betweenness Clustering for semantic propagation.
method_components: Graph Construction with pairwise label similarity
Adaptive Betweenness Clustering with binary cross-entropy loss
key_innovation: The integration of confidence-based node removal and prediction dissimilarity-based edge pruning to create a reliable graph structure before semantic propagation, addressing the unreliability of initial graph connectivity.
assumptions: Labeled samples across domains have trustworthy ground-truth labels
Features and labels vary smoothly over graph edges
High prediction confidence indicates reliable unlabeled samples
theoretical_grounding: Uses binary cross-entropy loss for clustering but provides no formal proofs or theoretical bounds.
hyperparameters: ξ0 (initial learning rate), α=0.03, β=25.0, τ=0.95, τ'=0.975, κ=0.20, temperature=0.05 (model), temperature=0.85 (sharpening), T (epochs, varies by dataset, common=100)
headline_result: G-ABC achieved 72.02% mean accuracy on Office-31 3-shot

ablations.impact: Removing reduces mean accuracy from 76.5% to 72.1% on DomainNet 3-shot
Removing (denoted †) reduces mean accuracy from 76.5% to 75.4% on DomainNet 3-shot
Removing (denoted ‡) reduces mean accuracy from 76.5% to 75.9% on DomainNet 3-shot

ablations.component: First positive term (sij log(pT i pj))
ablations.impact: Removing (denoted ♣) reduces mean accuracy from 76.5% to 75.6% on DomainNet 3-shot

ablations.component: Second negative term ((1−sij) log(1−pT i pj))
ablations.impact: Removing (denoted ♠) reduces mean accuracy from 76.5% to 74.9% on DomainNet 3-shot

improvement_over_baseline: G-ABC improves ECACL-T by 2.3% (AlexNet) and 3.1% (ResNet-34) on DomainNet 1-shot; outperforms MCL by 2.08% (ResNet-34) on DomainNet 3-shot
best_result: G-ABC achieved 72.02% mean accuracy on Office-31 3-shot
title: Adaptive Betweenness Clustering for Semi-Supervised Domain Adaptation
